channel.title: Dong Xing's Blog
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Dong Xing's Blog

item.title: Binary Search
item.description: Binary Search is an algorithm that counts the number of true values in an array of n booleans f_0,f_1,\dots,f_{n-1} satisfying \forall i\in\{1,2,\dots,n-1\},f_i\Rightarrow f_{i-1} in \mathcal{O}(\log n) time and \mathcal{O}(1) space.

item.title: Merge Sort
item.description: Merge Sort is an algorithm that sorts an array of n numbers a_0,a_1,\dots,a_{n-1} in non-decreasing order in \mathcal{O}(n\log n) time and \mathcal{O}(n) space.

item.title: Heapsort
item.description: Heapsort is an algorithm that sorts an array of n numbers a_0,a_1,\dots,a_{n-1} in non-decreasing order in \mathcal{O}(n\log n) time and \mathcal{O}(1) space.

item.title: Selection Sort
item.description: Selection Sort is an algorithm that sorts an array of n numbers a_0,a_1,\dots,a_{n-1} in non-decreasing order in \mathcal{O}(n^2) time and \mathcal{O}(1) space.

item.title: Insertion Sort
item.description: Insertion Sort is an algorithm that sorts an array of n numbers a_0,a_1,\dots,a_{n-1} in non-decreasing order in \mathcal{O}(n^2) time and \mathcal{O}(1) space.

item.title: Bubble Sort
item.description: Bubble Sort is an algorithm that sorts an array of n numbers a_0,a_1,\dots,a_{n-1} in non-decreasing order in \mathcal{O}(n^2) time and \mathcal{O}(1) space.

item.title: Tarjan's Bridge-Finding Algorithm
item.description: Tarjan’s Bridge-Finding Algorithm is an algorithm that finds the bridges in an undirected graph G=(V,E) in \mathcal{O}(|V|+|E|) time and \mathcal{O}(|V|+|E|) space.

item.title: Tarjan's Cut-Vertex-Finding Algorithm
item.description: Tarjan’s Cut-Vertex-Finding Algorithm is an algorithm that finds the cut vertices in an undirected graph G=(V,E) in \mathcal{O}(|V|+|E|) time and \mathcal{O}(|V|+|E|) space.

item.title: Tarjan's Strongly Connected Components Algorithm
item.description: Tarjan’s Strongly Connected Components Algorithm is an algorithm that finds the strongly connected components of a directed graph G=(V,E) in \mathcal{O}(|V|+|E|) and \mathcal{O}(|V|+|E|) space.

item.title: Bellman-Ford Algorithm
item.description: The Bellman-Ford Algorithm is an algorithm that computes the length of the shortest path from a vertex s to every vertex in a directed graph G=(V,E) with edge weights and no negative cycles in \mathcal{O}(|V||E|) time and \mathcal{O}(|V|) space.
